# Contributing

This repository contains source material for a blog series on AI-assisted software development published at [dczerwinski.pl](https://dczerwinski.pl). It is primarily a personal project, but suggestions and corrections are welcome.

## What you can contribute

- **Typos and factual errors** — open an issue or submit a PR directly
- **Broken links or outdated references** — open an issue
- **Code bugs in `/app`** — open an issue with steps to reproduce

## What is out of scope

- New features or post ideas — content decisions belong to the author
- Refactors or style changes unrelated to correctness

## How to submit a fix

1. Fork the repository
2. Create a branch: `fix/<short-description>`
3. Make your change
4. Open a pull request with a clear description of what you changed and why

## Commit sign-off

By submitting a contribution you certify that you wrote it or have the right to submit it under the Apache 2.0 license, and you agree to the [Developer Certificate of Origin](https://developercertificate.org/).

Add `Signed-off-by: Your Name <your@email.com>` to your commit message, or use `git commit -s`.
